title: Retire Bureau Cabinet bridge and frontier execution surfaces
goal: Remove the unused Cabinet graph, bridge, frontier, promotion and reviewed-import execution surfaces from Bureau after the Systemkatalog became static-only, while preserving historical registry evidence and Bureau's own queue, frontier and live-register authority.

approval.note: The operator explicitly instructed Grabowski to implement the Bureau cleanup. The effect is limited to reviewed Bureau source changes and removal of obsolete installed Bureau command wrappers after merge.

acceptance.assertion: T024 is merged into bureau:main before the implementation PR or local Bureau installation is changed.
The eight active src/bureau/cabinet_* graph, bridge, frontier, review, receipt, policy and promotion modules are absent from the current Bureau source tree.

The seven systemkatalog-* CLI subcommands that still implement Cabinet task-candidate, frontier, promotion or import semantics are absent, while Bureau's native frontier, queue, what-now and live-register commands remain unchanged.
The five bureau-systemkatalog-* console scripts are absent from package metadata and from ~/.local/bin after exact-commit deployment.
No active Bureau source, package configuration, systemd unit or current operations document refers to ~/repos/cabinet graph or bridge paths.

The former bridge import contract and policy are preserved under docs/archive/cabinet-era with explicit retired pointers at their previous paths; historical tasks, initiatives, plans and reports are not rewritten.
A repository validation gate rejects restoration of retired modules, CLI commands, console scripts or Cabinet default paths in active source and configuration.
Full Bureau lint, tests, registry validation and GitHub CI pass, and the normal Bureau CLI plus installed user services remain healthy after deployment.
T024 is closed with implementation merge, deployment identity, removed-command proof and validation evidence in a separately reviewed Bureau closeout.

rollback.trigger: A real external consumer of the retired commands is discovered before deployment completion or the normal Bureau CLI fails after package replacement.
rollback.procedure: Restore the previous immutable Bureau venv and wrapper set, verify Bureau checks and services, and stop T024 without rewriting historical evidence.

conditions: T024 is not yet present in bureau:origin/main
a foreign Bureau lease, dirty worktree, process or overlapping pull request is present
a timer, service, script or process is found to consume one of the retired commands
the implementation changes Bureau native queue, frontier, claim, what-now or live-register semantics
historical registry evidence or private Cabinet state would be deleted or rewritten
full validation or deployment verification fails

metadata.source_decision: After the static-only Systemkatalog retirement, the operator instructed Grabowski to remove Bureau's remaining active Cabinet execution logic rather than repoint it to the static catalog.

scope: remove the eight unused Bureau Cabinet execution modules
remove the seven misleading systemkatalog-* Cabinet-semantics subcommands
remove the five obsolete bureau-systemkatalog-* package and installed wrappers
archive the former bridge import contract and retain valid retired pointers
deploy the exact merged Bureau commit and verify normal Bureau commands and services
conditions: T024 is merged before implementation effect
all repository changes use an isolated worktree on current origin/main
no live timer, service, script, process or journal use of the retired commands exists
Bureau native coordination paths remain unchanged
historical and private Cabinet evidence remains preserved
a previous immutable Bureau deployment remains available for rollback
does_not_authorize: force-releasing foreign leases or changing foreign worktrees
deletion of private Cabinet databases, state directories, archives, backups or historical receipts
rewriting completed task, initiative, plan, report or pull request evidence
changes to Bureau queue, claim, native frontier, what-now, live-register or dispatch semantics
mutation of Systemkatalog or unrelated repositories

evidence_basis: installed Bureau CLI and wrapper inventory

does_not_establish: absence of every historical or external reference outside the audited host and repository
permission to delete private or historical state
future absence without a new live check
